Actionable initiatives this sweep: **15**.

Within each correctness class, initiatives are sorted by estimated context cost ASC (cheapest first). No heroic-last carries remain after deferring the two structural items above.

### 1. `roslyn-fetch-resource-timing` ‚Äî Diagnose `fetch_mcp_resource` "server not ready" on `roslyn://` URIs

**Status:** merged (PR #286, 2026-04-20) ¬∑ **Order:** 1 ¬∑ **Correctness class:** P3-correctness ¬∑ **Schedule hint:** ‚Äî ¬∑ **Estimated context:** 40000 tokens ¬∑ **CHANGELOG category:** Fixed

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `roslyn-fetch-resource-timing` |
| Diagnosis | Consumer reports intermittent `"server not ready"` when `fetch_mcp_resource` is called against `roslyn://` URIs in Cursor. Root cause not yet localized: candidate loci are (a) the resource handler's readiness-gate check racing `workspace_load` completion, (b) the stdio host emitting resource-list before workspace is ready, or (c) a doc gap in the Cursor-side expected call order. Resource handlers live under `src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Resources/` (exact file per source-verify pass). |
| Approach | (a) Read the `roslyn://` resource handler and the `WorkspaceExecutionGate` / `WorkspaceManager` readiness-status API. (b) Add a test that calls `fetch_mcp_resource` immediately after `workspace_load` returns and asserts it does not error. (c) If the readiness gate is wrong: fix by awaiting `WorkspaceManager.WaitForReadyAsync` (or equivalent) inside the resource handler before dispatching. (d) If the issue is a doc gap: update `ai_docs/runtime.md` with the required `workspace_load` ‚Üí resource-fetch ordering. Decide between (c) and (d) based on source reading. |
| Scope | prod: 1‚Äì2 (`src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Resources/*ResourceHandler.cs`; possibly `src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/WorkspaceManager.cs` readiness-gate accessor; possibly `ai_docs/runtime.md`). tests: +1 (`tests/RoslynMcp.Tests/Resources/FetchMcpResourceReadinessTests.cs` or extend an existing resource test file). |
| Tool policy | `edit-only` |
| Estimated context cost | 40000 tokens |
| Risks | If the race is genuinely in the host's resource dispatch (before the handler runs), the fix needs to land in `Program.cs` startup rather than in the handler, widening scope. Keep watchful for this. Second risk: flakiness in the new regression test if it depends on wall-clock timing ‚Äî use explicit readiness-await, not `Task.Delay`. |
| Validation | New unit test passes 100√ó. Manual repro via Cursor: load a solution, immediately issue `fetch_mcp_resource roslyn://<workspace>/‚Ä¶`, assert no `"server not ready"` error in three back-to-back attempts. Existing resource tests still pass. |
| Performance review | N/A ‚Äî correctness fix, no hot-path changes. |
| CHANGELOG category | Fixed |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Fixed:** `fetch_mcp_resource` no longer returns `"server not ready"` for `roslyn://` URIs when called immediately after `workspace_load` ‚Äî the resource handler now awaits `WorkspaceManager` readiness before dispatching (`roslyn-fetch-resource-timing`). |
| Backlog sync | Close row: `roslyn-fetch-resource-timing`. |

### 2. `ci-verify-release-coverage-on-main-only` ‚Äî Skip coverlet coverage on PRs; collect only on main

**Status:** merged (PR #285, 2026-04-20) ¬∑ **Order:** 2 ¬∑ **Correctness class:** P3-UX ¬∑ **Schedule hint:** ‚Äî ¬∑ **Estimated context:** 30000 tokens ¬∑ **CHANGELOG category:** Changed

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `ci-verify-release-coverage-on-main-only` |
| Diagnosis | `eng/verify-release.ps1:55` unconditionally runs `dotnet test --collect:"XPlat Code Coverage"`, triggering coverlet IL-rewrite per test assembly every PR. `CI_POLICY.md` treats coverage as informational (artifact + ReportGenerator HTML), not a merge gate ‚Äî so the PR-time collection is pure latency. `.github/workflows/ci.yml` already has a `detect-docs-only` gate; extending the pattern to also gate coverage on `push` vs `pull_request` is clean. |
| Approach | (a) Add `[switch]$NoCoverage` parameter to `eng/verify-release.ps1`; when set, omit the `--collect` flag from the `dotnet test` invocation. (b) In `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, pass `-NoCoverage` when `github.event_name == 'pull_request'`; full collection remains on push-to-main + scheduled + workflow_dispatch. Gate the "Generate coverage HTML summary" and "Upload code coverage" steps on the same condition. (c) Update `CI_POLICY.md` ¬ß Repository Evidence with the PR-vs-main split. |
| Scope | prod: 3 (`eng/verify-release.ps1`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `CI_POLICY.md`). tests: 0 (CI-pipeline change; validated by CI itself). |
| Tool policy | `edit-only` |
| Estimated context cost | 30000 tokens |
| Risks | A first-time contributor may expect coverage on their PR and be confused by its absence ‚Äî update CI_POLICY.md explicitly. Second risk: if any existing workflow step consumes the coverage output on PR events, gating upload will break it ‚Äî grep `.github/workflows/` for coverage-artifact consumers before landing. |
| Validation | Open a dummy PR, confirm `validate` job skips coverage collection (step output shows no `--collect` flag). Merge to main, confirm full coverage is collected and uploaded. Check `CI_POLICY.md` renders cleanly. |
| Performance review | Saves 1‚Äì2 min per PR validate run (coverlet IL-rewrite dominant); no runtime impact. |
| CHANGELOG category | Changed |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Changed:** CI skips code-coverage collection on `pull_request` events ‚Äî coverage now runs only on push-to-main and scheduled runs, saving ~1‚Äì2 minutes of coverlet IL-rewrite per PR. `CI_POLICY.md` ¬ß Repository Evidence updated to reflect the PR-vs-main split (`ci-verify-release-coverage-on-main-only`). |
| Backlog sync | Close row: `ci-verify-release-coverage-on-main-only`. |

### 3. `gh-pr-merge-from-worktree-fails-uses-local-main` ‚Äî Document worktree-cd discipline for `gh pr merge`

**Status:** merged (PR #283, 2026-04-20) ¬∑ **Order:** 3 ¬∑ **Correctness class:** P4 ¬∑ **Schedule hint:** ‚Äî ¬∑ **Estimated context:** 20000 tokens ¬∑ **CHANGELOG category:** Maintenance

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `gh-pr-merge-from-worktree-fails-uses-local-main` |
| Diagnosis | `gh pr merge <n> --squash --delete-branch` fails with `fatal: 'main' is already used by worktree at ‚Ä¶` when invoked from inside a git worktree because `gh` attempts to update/delete the local branch against the primary-repo checkout. The `/ship` skill is external (lives at `~/.claude/skills/ship/SKILL.md`, not in this repo), and Option (d) from the backlog row (documenting in `backlog-sweep-execute.md` Step 8) has already shipped. The remaining in-repo fix is Option (a): capture the discipline in `ai_docs/workflow.md` so any repo-local reader sees it. |
| Approach | Add a subsection "Worktree + `gh pr merge` discipline" to `ai_docs/workflow.md` under ¬ß Post-Merge Cleanup (between the existing bullets and ¬ß Ownership). Content: state the failure mode, give the one-liner `cd "$(git rev-parse --git-common-dir)/.."` as the fix, and cross-reference `ai_docs/prompts/backlog-sweep-execute.md` Step 8. No code, no hooks. |
| Scope | prod: 1 (`ai_docs/workflow.md`). tests: 0. |
| Tool policy | `edit-only` |
| Estimated context cost | 20000 tokens |
| Risks | Minimal ‚Äî doc-only. Keep the note terse (‚â§ 8 lines) so `ai_docs/workflow.md` stays under 60 lines. |
| Validation | `ai_docs/workflow.md` renders cleanly; the new section is linkable via anchor `#worktree-gh-pr-merge-discipline`; no other docs reference the now-shadowed pattern without cross-linking here. |
| Performance review | N/A ‚Äî doc change. |
| CHANGELOG category | Maintenance |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Maintenance:** `ai_docs/workflow.md` documents the `cd "$(git rev-parse --git-common-dir)/.."` preamble required before `gh pr merge` from inside a git worktree; eliminates the obscure `fatal: 'main' is already used by worktree` failure that bit every worktree-origin merge (`gh-pr-merge-from-worktree-fails-uses-local-main`). |
| Backlog sync | Close row: `gh-pr-merge-from-worktree-fails-uses-local-main`. |

### 5. `windows-testhost-file-lock-blocks-worktree-cleanup` ‚Äî Add `dotnet build-server shutdown` to worktree cleanup

**Status:** merged (PR #288, 2026-04-20) ¬∑ **Order:** 5 ¬∑ **Correctness class:** P4 ¬∑ **Schedule hint:** ‚Äî ¬∑ **Estimated context:** 20000 tokens ¬∑ **CHANGELOG category:** Maintenance

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `windows-testhost-file-lock-blocks-worktree-cleanup` |
| Diagnosis | After a subagent runs `./eng/verify-release.ps1` in a worktree, `testhost.exe` and `VBCSCompiler.exe` retain handles on `tests/RoslynMcp.Tests/bin/{Debug,Release}/net10.0`, making `rm -rf .worktrees/<id>` fail with `Device or resource busy`. Hit twice in 2026-04-18 pass 3. `ai_docs/prompts/backlog-sweep-execute.md` Appendix A step 8 is the documented cleanup site (lines 545‚Äì553 per source-verify); Step 5b is not present ‚Äî the row's original reference was to a step that has since been consolidated into Appendix A. |
| Approach | Edit `ai_docs/prompts/backlog-sweep-execute.md` Appendix A step 8 (worktree-cleanup section) to prepend `dotnet build-server shutdown` before any `git worktree remove` / `rm -rf .worktrees/<id>` command. The SDK command is a no-op when no servers are running, so it is always safe. Also add a short note in ¬ß Step 5b (or wherever the current cleanup-step contract lives) if that section still exists; skip if already consolidated. |
| Scope | prod: 1 (`ai_docs/prompts/backlog-sweep-execute.md`). tests: 0. |
| Tool policy | `edit-only` |
| Estimated context cost | 20000 tokens |
| Risks | `dotnet build-server shutdown` prints a line to stdout; ensure the prompt's cleanup pattern doesn't treat that as an error. Low risk overall ‚Äî the command is recommended by Microsoft SDK docs for exactly this scenario. |
| Validation | Next backlog-sweep session runs verify-release in a worktree and the cleanup phase removes the worktree directory without residue. No `testhost.exe` processes remain after cleanup (check `tasklist /FI "IMAGENAME eq testhost.exe"`). |
| Performance review | N/A ‚Äî cleanup-scope fix. |
| CHANGELOG category | Maintenance |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Maintenance:** `ai_docs/prompts/backlog-sweep-execute.md` Appendix A step 8 prepends `dotnet build-server shutdown` to worktree cleanup, eliminating the Windows `testhost.exe` / `VBCSCompiler.exe` file-lock residue that left stale `.worktrees/*` directories after every verify-release run (`windows-testhost-file-lock-blocks-worktree-cleanup`). |
| Backlog sync | Close row: `windows-testhost-file-lock-blocks-worktree-cleanup`. |

### 6. `ci-skip-dotnet-test-on-docs-only-prs` ‚Äî Expand docs-only regex; audit branch-protection

**Status:** merged (PR #291, 2026-04-20) ¬∑ **Order:** 6 ¬∑ **Correctness class:** P4 ¬∑ **Schedule hint:** ‚Äî ¬∑ **Estimated context:** 20000 tokens ¬∑ **CHANGELOG category:** Maintenance

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `ci-skip-dotnet-test-on-docs-only-prs` |
| Diagnosis | `.github/workflows/ci.yml:23‚Äì45` already has `detect-docs-only` with regex `^(.*\.md\|ai_docs/.*\.json)$` gating build/test/coverage/artifact steps (source-verified; lines 48, 57, 70, 75, 88, 93, 100 all gated). Remaining work per the row: (a) confirm branch-protection treats the skipped `validate` job as passing (not skipped-blocking); (b) audit whether `ai_docs/**/*.json` is a safe skip pattern against future build inputs; (c) consider expanding to `.claude/**/*.md` and `skills/**/*.md` now that the 2026-04-18 evidence base is stable. |
| Approach | (a) Read current `.github/workflows/ci.yml` and confirm the `if: steps.detect.outputs.docs_only != 'true'` gates. (b) Verify branch-protection by checking `gh api /repos/:owner/:repo/branches/main/protection` output and confirming the `validate` check is required (the skipped steps produce green). (c) Expand the docs-only regex to include `.claude/.*\.md$` and `skills/.*\.md$` ‚Äî both are shipped-artifact directories but edits to them need no compile/test. (d) Document in `CI_POLICY.md` that the pattern is `^(.*\.md\|ai_docs/.*\.json)$` AS OF this change, and list the exact excluded paths so future additions are visible. |
| Scope | prod: 2 (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `CI_POLICY.md`). tests: 0. |
| Tool policy | `edit-only` |
| Estimated context cost | 20000 tokens |
| Risks | If branch-protection currently requires the skipped steps individually (not via the umbrella `validate` check), the expansion could inadvertently block doc PRs. Verify branch-protection API output before landing. Second risk: `skills/**/*.md` is the shipped Roslyn plugin surface ‚Äî an edit there changes the installed plugin content, so the pattern must still not skip publish-preflight-relevant steps. Cross-check that `publish-preflight` is NOT gated by docs-only. |
| Validation | Open a dummy PR that only touches `.claude/*.md` ‚Äî confirm the `validate` job reports green with no build/test steps executed. Open a second PR touching a `.cs` file ‚Äî confirm build/test still run. Branch-protection accepts both PRs as passing. |
| Performance review | Saves ~10 min of CI time per docs-only PR; 40 min+ saved per typical backlog-sweep session (4+ reconcile PRs). |
| CHANGELOG category | Maintenance |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Maintenance:** CI's docs-only skip pattern now covers `.claude/**/*.md` and `skills/**/*.md` in addition to top-level `.md` and `ai_docs/**/*.json`, eliminating ~10 minutes of redundant `validate` wall time per doc-only PR. Branch-protection configuration audited; `CI_POLICY.md` ¬ß Repository Evidence lists the full excluded-paths regex (`ci-skip-dotnet-test-on-docs-only-prs`). |
| Backlog sync | Close row: `ci-skip-dotnet-test-on-docs-only-prs`. |

### 7. `plan-anchor-examples-go-stale-between-plan-and-execute` ‚Äî Planner-side `verifyAnchors` step

**Status:** merged (PR #289, 2026-04-20) ¬∑ **Order:** 7 ¬∑ **Correctness class:** P4 ¬∑ **Schedule hint:** ‚Äî ¬∑ **Estimated context:** 25000 tokens ¬∑ **CHANGELOG category:** Maintenance

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `plan-anchor-examples-go-stale-between-plan-and-execute` |
| Diagnosis | Backlog-sweep plans cite specific symbol/file/method anchors in the Validation field; between plan-draft and plan-execute, other initiatives can close the very scenario the plan anchored to. Pass-3 init 6 hit this with `Build*Diff` helpers that had consolidated into `DiffGenerator.GenerateUnifiedDiff`. `ai_docs/prompts/backlog-sweep-plan.md:198‚Äì213` is the Step 3 "Independent verification" section ‚Äî the natural host for a new verifyAnchors sub-step. |
| Approach | Add a bullet to Step 3 of `ai_docs/prompts/backlog-sweep-plan.md`: "**Anchor verification.** For each symbol/method name cited in an initiative's Validation or Diagnosis field, run `mcp__roslyn__symbol_search` (or equivalent Grep) at plan-draft time. If the anchor does not resolve, rewrite the field to `[stale ‚Äî cited anchor not found; executor may use synthetic examples]` rather than dropping the initiative. Plans still ship; the executor is informed up front." Also add a reminder to the Step 6 self-vet checklist: "[ ] Anchors in Validation/Diagnosis fields still resolve (Step 3 anchor-verification pass complete)." |
| Scope | prod: 1 (`ai_docs/prompts/backlog-sweep-plan.md`). tests: 0. |
| Tool policy | `edit-only` |
| Estimated context cost | 25000 tokens |
| Risks | The verifyAnchors pass itself adds planner context cost (~2K tokens per initiative with cited anchors). Budget-sensitive sweeps could skip it, but the prompt should make it SHOULD not MUST, with a note that skipping in exchange for cost is explicit. Keep the bullet opinionated but not dogmatic. |
| Validation | Next plan-draft pass includes the anchor-verification step visibly (grep the new plan.md for "Anchor verification" or similar). A test-deck plan that intentionally cites a nonexistent `Foo.Bar` method triggers the `[stale]` rewrite in the Validation field. |
| Performance review | N/A ‚Äî prompt-engineering change. |
| CHANGELOG category | Maintenance |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Maintenance:** `ai_docs/prompts/backlog-sweep-plan.md` Step 3 gains a "verify anchors" sub-step ‚Äî planners now `symbol_search` each cited symbol/method name at plan-draft time and rewrite stale anchors to `[stale ‚Äî cited anchor not found]` rather than shipping an execution-time surprise. Prevents recurrence of the 2026-04-18 pass-3 init-6 stale-anchor pivot (`plan-anchor-examples-go-stale-between-plan-and-execute`). |
| Backlog sync | Close row: `plan-anchor-examples-go-stale-between-plan-and-execute`. |

### 8. `audit-remaining-pretooluse-hook-blocks-after-pr-230` ‚Äî Cold-subagent-aware hook accept-list

**Status:** merged (PR #290, 2026-04-20) ¬∑ **Order:** 8 ¬∑ **Correctness class:** P4 ¬∑ **Schedule hint:** ‚Äî ¬∑ **Estimated context:** 25000 tokens ¬∑ **CHANGELOG category:** Fixed

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `audit-remaining-pretooluse-hook-blocks-after-pr-230` |
| Diagnosis | `hooks/hooks.json:5` matches 19 `*_apply` tools and the prompt at line 9 currently accepts EITHER (a) a direct matching `*_preview` earlier in the conversation, OR (b) an `apply_composite_preview` redemption. Cold-subagent sessions have NO prior-turn preview ‚Äî a fresh subagent that needs `rename_apply` immediately legitimately violates (a) and (b), and the current prompt has no "same-turn preview-then-apply" escape. 2 of 8 cold subagents in passes 1+2 stalled with `"The hook is being conservative..."`. |
| Approach | Edit the `hooks/hooks.json:5` prompt (line 9) to add a third accept case: "(c) A matching `*_preview` call appears in the CURRENT agent turn immediately before this `*_apply` ‚Äî same-turn preview-then-apply is the primary v3 parallel-mode flow and MUST NOT be blocked even in cold-subagent sessions with no prior conversation." Also extend the examples block with one same-turn case. Keep the block semantics for stale / missing previews intact. |
| Scope | prod: 1 (`hooks/hooks.json`). tests: 0 (hook prompts are not unit-testable in this repo; validation is via session-level evidence). |
| Tool policy | `edit-only` |
| Estimated context cost | 25000 tokens |
| Risks | Loosening the accept-list could let genuinely-abandoned previews through. Mitigation: the new case is specifically "SAME TURN" not "any turn before," which is the strictest possible accept expansion. Second risk: the hook prompt is a single JSON string with escape-sensitive newlines ‚Äî keep the edit anchored on the existing prompt text and re-validate JSON shape before commit. |
| Validation | Manual: open a worktree-scoped cold-subagent session, have it call `rename_preview` then `rename_apply` in the same turn; assert the hook responds `proceed`. Existing back-to-back same-session preview+apply flows continue to pass. |
| Performance review | N/A ‚Äî hook prompt change. |
| CHANGELOG category | Fixed |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Fixed:** The PreToolUse `*_apply` hook accepts same-turn `*_preview`‚Üí`*_apply` as valid preview evidence; cold-subagent sessions (parallel backlog-sweep worktrees) no longer stall on the hook's "no prior-turn preview" false-positive block. Fixes the 25% cold-subagent failure rate seen in 2026-04-17 passes 1 and 2 (`audit-remaining-pretooluse-hook-blocks-after-pr-230`). |
| Backlog sync | Close row: `audit-remaining-pretooluse-hook-blocks-after-pr-230`. |

### 9. `workspace-health-triage-subagent` ‚Äî New `.claude/agents/workspace-health-triage.md`

**Status:** pending ¬∑ **Order:** 9 ¬∑ **Correctness class:** P4 ¬∑ **Schedule hint:** ‚Äî ¬∑ **Estimated context:** 25000 tokens ¬∑ **CHANGELOG category:** Added

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `workspace-health-triage-subagent` |
| Diagnosis | Answering "is the Roslyn workspace loaded and healthy?" today burns 56 Bash + 16 Grep + 26 Read calls (per 2026-04-16 session evidence) in the orchestrator's main context. A dedicated subagent runs the sequence in isolation and returns a structured summary. Sibling agents at `.claude/agents/` (initiative-executor, pr-reconciler, changelog-and-backlog-sync) give the template: YAML frontmatter (name, description, model, tools) + markdown body with numbered steps. |
| Approach | Create `.claude/agents/workspace-health-triage.md` mirroring the sibling-agent template. Frontmatter: `name: workspace-health-triage`, short description, `model: sonnet`, `tools: mcp__roslyn__workspace_list,mcp__roslyn__workspace_health,mcp__roslyn__server_info,mcp__roslyn__workspace_reload`. Body: 4 numbered steps (workspace_list ‚Üí workspace_health per workspace ‚Üí server_info for version/count ‚Üí optional workspace_reload on drift). Output contract: single STATUS-block of 6 lines (STATUS, VERSION, TOOL_COUNT, WORKSPACES, ACTIONS_TAKEN, NOTES). |
| Scope | prod: 1 (`.claude/agents/workspace-health-triage.md`). tests: 0. |
| Tool policy | `edit-only` |
| Estimated context cost | 25000 tokens |
| Risks | Writing the subagent prompt thin enough that the orchestrator actually benefits ‚Äî if it becomes a verbose 200-line brief, it just moves the context cost. Target: ‚â§ 80 lines body. Second risk: the tool whitelist is over-narrow and the subagent can't diagnose odd failure modes ‚Äî add an escape for "call any read-side mcp__roslyn__ tool if state is ambiguous." |
| Validation | Invoke the subagent from a session with a healthy workspace; assert 6-line output. Invoke with no workspace loaded; assert STATUS: unloaded. Invoke with a stale workspace; assert ACTIONS_TAKEN notes the reload. |
| Performance review | N/A ‚Äî agent-authoring change. |
| CHANGELOG category | Added |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Added:** `.claude/agents/workspace-health-triage.md` subagent encapsulates the `workspace_list` ‚Üí `workspace_health` ‚Üí `server_info` ‚Üí conditional `workspace_reload` sequence into a single delegation that returns a 6-line STATUS block, dropping the orchestrator's workspace-health context cost from ~100 tool-result lines to ~8 (`workspace-health-triage-subagent`). |
| Backlog sync | Close row: `workspace-health-triage-subagent`. |

### 10. `subagent-malformed-result-orphans-uncommitted-work` ‚Äî New `/recover-stalled-subagent` skill

**Status:** pending ¬∑ **Order:** 10 ¬∑ **Correctness class:** P4 ¬∑ **Schedule hint:** ‚Äî ¬∑ **Estimated context:** 30000 tokens ¬∑ **CHANGELOG category:** Added

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `subagent-malformed-result-orphans-uncommitted-work` |
| Diagnosis | Cold-subagent stalls (~1 in 10 across 2026-04-17+18 passes) can leave uncommitted work stranded in an abandoned worktree when the subagent emits a thought-fragment final message without the `<<<RESULT>>>` sentinel. 2026-04-18 pass-3 init-5 stranded 729 insertions across 4 prod files + 1 test before recovery. Current `ai_docs/prompts/backlog-sweep-execute.md` Appendix A documents detection (line ~526) but no recovery path; Appendix C defines malformed-result detection but stops there. |
| Approach | Create `.claude/skills/recover-stalled-subagent/SKILL.md` invocable as `/recover-stalled-subagent <branch-name>`. Steps: (a) derive worktree path from branch name ‚Äî typically `.worktrees/<id>`; (b) `cd` into worktree, run `git status --porcelain`; (c) if work exists: stage with explicit paths only (NO `git add -A`), commit as `wip(<scope>): <branch>-partial (subagent stalled)`, push to origin; (d) `cd` back to primary repo, `git worktree remove --force`, `dotnet build-server shutdown` first if applicable; (e) emit a 6-line STATE_HANDOFF block for the orchestrator's state.json `notes` field. Follow the repo-local skill template at `.claude/skills/close-backlog-rows/SKILL.md`. |
| Scope | prod: 1 (`.claude/skills/recover-stalled-subagent/SKILL.md`). tests: 0 (skill is operator-invoked; validation is session evidence). |
| Tool policy | `edit-only` |
| Estimated context cost | 30000 tokens |
| Risks | Explicit-path staging regresses if the stall produced a file the skill didn't enumerate ‚Äî start from `git status --porcelain`'s full list, not a pre-declared allow-list. Second risk: `git worktree remove --force` on a path that still holds a lock (see initiative 5). Solution: initiative 5's `dotnet build-server shutdown` discipline is a hard prerequisite documented in the new skill. |
| Validation | Manual: contrive a stall (kill a subagent mid-turn), run `/recover-stalled-subagent remediation/foo`, confirm all WIP is committed to `remediation/foo` on origin and the worktree directory is removed cleanly. |
| Performance review | N/A ‚Äî skill-authoring change. |
| CHANGELOG category | Added |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Added:** `/recover-stalled-subagent` skill at `.claude/skills/recover-stalled-subagent/SKILL.md` ‚Äî recovers a stalled initiative-executor subagent's uncommitted worktree into a WIP branch + cleans the worktree in one invocation instead of the 3-turn manual recovery dance observed in 2026-04-18 pass 3 (`subagent-malformed-result-orphans-uncommitted-work`). |
| Backlog sync | Close row: `subagent-malformed-result-orphans-uncommitted-work`. |

### 11. `readme-surface-counts-drift-from-live-catalog` ‚Äî Regression test for README vs `ServerSurfaceCatalog`

**Status:** pending ¬∑ **Order:** 11 ¬∑ **Correctness class:** P4 ¬∑ **Schedule hint:** ‚Äî ¬∑ **Estimated context:** 30000 tokens ¬∑ **CHANGELOG category:** Added

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `readme-surface-counts-drift-from-live-catalog` |
| Diagnosis | `README.md:282` advertises "147 tools (104 stable / 43 experimental), 13 resources (9 stable / 4 experimental), 20 prompts (all experimental)"; `src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Catalog/ServerSurfaceCatalog.cs` (447 lines, non-partial) is the live ground truth but has no build/test-time gate enforcing README agreement. The existing `ServerSurfaceCatalogAnalyzer` (RMCP001/RMCP002) checks catalog vs `[McpServerTool]` attributes ‚Äî not catalog vs README. The row's Option (d) (a unit test) is the minimal-friction fix. |
| Approach | Create `tests/RoslynMcp.Tests/ReadmeSurfaceCountTests.cs`: (a) load `ServerSurfaceCatalog.Tools` / `.Resources` / `.Prompts` via reflection at test time; count entries by `SupportTier` (stable / experimental); (b) read `README.md` text, regex-extract the three "(X stable / Y experimental)" and "(Z tools)" numbers; (c) assert parsed = computed for each of tools/resources/prompts. On mismatch, the failure message names which count is off and by how much. If current README is drifted, update `README.md` in the same PR to match live counts before landing the test. |
| Scope | prod: 0‚Äì1 (`README.md` IF current counts drift from live catalog after the test is first run). tests: +1 (`tests/RoslynMcp.Tests/ReadmeSurfaceCountTests.cs`). |
| Tool policy | `edit-only` |
| Estimated context cost | 30000 tokens |
| Risks | README format is narrative English ‚Äî a wording change that preserves the numeric pattern "(X stable / Y experimental)" doesn't break the test, but a future README refactor that moves to a table could. Use a flexible regex and commit a golden-comment next to the regex explaining why. |
| Validation | Run the new test in isolation via `dotnet test --filter FullyQualifiedName~ReadmeSurfaceCountTests`; should pass after any needed README update. `verify-release.ps1` picks it up automatically. Introduce a deliberate drift (add a fake `stable` tool to the catalog in a feature branch); the test fails with a clear "README stable tool count: 104, catalog: 105" message. |
| Performance review | N/A ‚Äî test addition, no hot path. |
| CHANGELOG category | Added |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Added:** `tests/RoslynMcp.Tests/ReadmeSurfaceCountTests.cs` ‚Äî asserts `README.md`'s "(X stable / Y experimental)" counts for tools, resources, and prompts match the live `ServerSurfaceCatalog` at test time. Closes the README-vs-catalog drift blind spot that left v1.22.0 advertising an off-by-one tool count (`readme-surface-counts-drift-from-live-catalog`). |
| Backlog sync | Close row: `readme-surface-counts-drift-from-live-catalog`. |

### 12. `plan-vetter-subagent` ‚Äî New `.claude/agents/plan-vetter.md`

**Status:** pending ¬∑ **Order:** 12 ¬∑ **Correctness class:** P4 ¬∑ **Schedule hint:** ‚Äî ¬∑ **Estimated context:** 35000 tokens ¬∑ **CHANGELOG category:** Added

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `plan-vetter-subagent` |
| Diagnosis | Every backlog-sweep opens with 40‚Äì60 orchestrator tool-calls doing Step 0 + 1a preflight (git status, reconcile check, `state.json.schemaVersion == 2`, Rules 1/3/4/5 per-initiative validation). The pattern is identical session over session ‚Äî prime subagent material. Existing `.claude/agents/*.md` (initiative-executor.md, pr-reconciler.md, changelog-and-backlog-sync.md) define the shape. |
| Approach | Create `.claude/agents/plan-vetter.md`: frontmatter `name: plan-vetter`, `description: Pre-execution plan vetting ‚Äî reads state.json + plan.md, runs Rules 1/3/4/5, emits a compact go/no-go report with per-initiative flags.`, `model: sonnet`, `tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Bash`. Body steps: (1) load `state.json` and `plan.md`; (2) schema version check; (3) per-initiative Rule 1/3/4/5/5-cost vetting; (4) emit a 1-line-per-initiative PASS/FAIL table + a summary verdict (SKIP_INITIATIVES: [...]). Input contract: `planPath` (required). Output contract: structured VERDICT block. |
| Scope | prod: 1 (`.claude/agents/plan-vetter.md`). tests: 0. |
| Tool policy | `edit-only` |
| Estimated context cost | 35000 tokens |
| Risks | Duplicates the prompt's Step 0+1a logic in a subagent; if the prompt changes (e.g. Rule 3 gets a new exemption), the subagent drifts. Mitigation: the subagent prompt explicitly says "authoritative rule list is in `ai_docs/prompts/backlog-sweep-plan.md` and `backlog-sweep-execute.md`; read those at invocation time rather than inlining." Second risk: if invoked on a malformed plan.md, the agent should fail fast with a clear message rather than attempting heroic recovery. |
| Validation | Invoke the subagent against this sweep's own plan.md. Expected output: PASS table + no SKIP_INITIATIVES. Invoke against a deliberately broken plan.md (e.g. initiative with `productionFilesTouched: 9`); expected: FAIL for that initiative, SKIP_INITIATIVES list includes it. |
| Performance review | N/A ‚Äî agent-authoring change. Saves ~50 tool calls per sweep. |
| CHANGELOG category | Added |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Added:** `.claude/agents/plan-vetter.md` subagent ‚Äî encapsulates the backlog-sweep-execute Step 0 + 1a preflight (schema-version check, per-initiative Rules 1/3/4/5 validation) into a single delegation that returns a compact go/no-go verdict, replacing the 40‚Äì60 orchestrator tool-calls observed in every 2026-04-16‚Üí17 sweep (`plan-vetter-subagent`). |
| Backlog sync | Close row: `plan-vetter-subagent`. |

### 13. `release-cut-atomic-skill-bump-ship-tag-reinstall` ‚Äî New `/release-cut` skill

**Status:** pending ¬∑ **Order:** 13 ¬∑ **Correctness class:** P4 ¬∑ **Schedule hint:** ‚Äî ¬∑ **Estimated context:** 35000 tokens ¬∑ **CHANGELOG category:** Added

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `release-cut-atomic-skill-bump-ship-tag-reinstall` |
| Diagnosis | Cutting a release today sequences 4 discrete user intents (bump ‚Üí ship ‚Üí tag ‚Üí reinstall-both-layers) with no shared error handling. 2026-04-17 v1.22.0 and 2026-04-18 v1.23.0 sessions each hit Layer-2 gaps (plugin marketplace cache stale) because `dotnet publish -p:ReinstallTool=true` alone doesn't refresh `~/.claude/plugins/cache/‚Ä¶`. Primitives that exist today: `.claude/skills/bump/SKILL.md` (69 lines), shipped `/roslyn-mcp:update` (both layers), `eng/update-claude-plugin.ps1` (169 lines), `eng/verify-version-drift.ps1`. External: `/ship` (global user skill at `~/.claude/skills/ship/SKILL.md`). |
| Approach | Create `.claude/skills/release-cut/SKILL.md` invocable as `/release-cut <major\|minor\|patch>`. Steps: (1) verify clean tree + up-to-date main; (2) `/bump <type>`; (3) `verify-release.ps1`; (4) `/ship` for commit+PR+squash-merge; (5) pull main, `git tag -a vX.Y.Z -m '...'`, `git push origin vX.Y.Z`; (6) delegate reinstall to `/roslyn-mcp:update` (which already wraps both layers) instead of re-implementing; (7) verify `server_info.version` reports new version AND `~/.claude/plugins/cache/roslyn-mcp-marketplace/roslyn-mcp/` shows only the new version. Each step records its output so a mid-flow failure is re-runnable from the last checkpoint. |
| Scope | prod: 1 (`.claude/skills/release-cut/SKILL.md`). tests: 0 (operator-invoked; validation is the next release session). |
| Tool policy | `edit-only` |
| Estimated context cost | 35000 tokens |
| Risks | Delegating to `/ship` is a cross-skill dependency ‚Äî if `/ship` changes its interface (it's a user-global skill, not version-pinned to this repo), the `/release-cut` skill silently breaks. Mitigation: the skill documents the exact `/ship` contract it assumes AND falls back to the manual `git commit; git push -u origin ‚Ä¶; gh pr create --squash` sequence when `/ship` is unavailable. Second risk: Shell-quoting `-p:` vs `/p:` bites on bash-on-Windows ‚Äî pin `-p:ReinstallTool=true` inside the skill once. |
| Validation | Next release cut (1.26.0 or later) uses `/release-cut patch`; flow completes end-to-end with both layers refreshed. Zero `stale plugin cache` warnings post-release. |
| Performance review | N/A ‚Äî skill-authoring change. |
| CHANGELOG category | Added |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Added:** `/release-cut` skill at `.claude/skills/release-cut/SKILL.md` ‚Äî single-invocation release pipeline (bump ‚Üí verify ‚Üí ship ‚Üí tag ‚Üí reinstall-both-layers) that delegates to `/bump`, `/ship`, and `/roslyn-mcp:update` with checkpointed step output. Closes the 2026-04-17 + 2026-04-18 two-session Layer-2-gap pattern where `dotnet publish -p:ReinstallTool=true` alone left the plugin marketplace cache stale (`release-cut-atomic-skill-bump-ship-tag-reinstall`). |
| Backlog sync | Close row: `release-cut-atomic-skill-bump-ship-tag-reinstall`. |

### 14. `parallel-pr-changelog-append-friction` ‚Äî Changelog-fragment directory pattern

**Status:** pending ¬∑ **Order:** 14 ¬∑ **Correctness class:** P4 ¬∑ **Schedule hint:** ‚Äî ¬∑ **Estimated context:** 50000 tokens ¬∑ **CHANGELOG category:** Changed

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `parallel-pr-changelog-append-friction` |
| Diagnosis | Parallel-subagent backlog-sweeps routinely hit `CHANGELOG.md` merge conflicts ‚Äî 2 of 4 subagents in 2026-04-17 pass-1 required a rebase+resolve on `CHANGELOG.md`. Option (a) (an idempotent MCP tool) is a bigger lift; Option (b) ‚Äî a changelog-fragment directory (towncrier/news-fragments style) ‚Äî is the architecturally correct conflict-free path since no two PRs ever touch the same fragment file. Existing primitives: `.claude/skills/draft-changelog-entry/SKILL.md` (132 lines), `.claude/skills/bump/SKILL.md` (69 lines), `CHANGELOG.md`. |
| Approach | (a) Create `changelog.d/` directory with a `README.md` explaining the per-fragment-file pattern; filenames `<row-id>.md`, contents = a single bullet. Add `.gitkeep` equivalent. (b) Update `.claude/skills/draft-changelog-entry/SKILL.md`: instead of editing `CHANGELOG.md`'s `## [Unreleased]` subsections, the skill now writes `changelog.d/<row-id>.md` with a YAML frontmatter carrying the category (Added/Changed/Fixed/Maintenance) + the bullet body. Merge-safe by construction. (c) Update `.claude/skills/bump/SKILL.md`: release-cut time, consume `changelog.d/*.md`, group by category frontmatter, emit into the new `## [X.Y.Z]` section of `CHANGELOG.md`, then `git rm` the consumed fragments in the same commit. (d) One-time migration: leave existing `## [Unreleased]` content as-is; new fragments start accumulating from the next merged PR forward. |
| Scope | prod: 4 (`changelog.d/README.md` (new), `.claude/skills/draft-changelog-entry/SKILL.md`, `.claude/skills/bump/SKILL.md`, `CHANGELOG.md` minor note pointing to `changelog.d/` for new entries). tests: 0 (skill-behavior validation is the next sweep's conflict-free merge). |
| Tool policy | `edit-only` |
| Estimated context cost | 50000 tokens |
| Risks | A fragment file with malformed frontmatter breaks the bump-time consumer; the bump skill must report invalid fragments without crashing. Second risk: fragment file naming collision ‚Äî if two initiatives use the same `<row-id>` filename (rare but possible for bundled work), the second `git add` overwrites the first. Mitigation: bump-skill disambiguates by appending `-2`, `-3` suffixes. Third risk: agents forget the new flow and fall back to editing `CHANGELOG.md` directly ‚Äî mitigate by updating `ai_docs/prompts/backlog-sweep-execute.md` Step 4 subagent-brief snippet to cite the new skill path. |
| Validation | Next parallel-sweep wave: 3+ subagents each add a `changelog.d/<row>.md` fragment; zero `CHANGELOG.md` merge conflicts. Release cut consumes all fragments into a single `## [X.Y.Z]` section and deletes them. Diff is grouped by category. |
| Performance review | Saves ~60s per rebase√óN subagents per sweep ‚Äî ~5‚Äì10 min per multi-initiative parallel session at steady state. |
| CHANGELOG category | Changed |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Changed:** Changelog workflow moves to a `changelog.d/` fragment-file pattern (towncrier-style) ‚Äî every PR writes `changelog.d/<row-id>.md` instead of appending to `CHANGELOG.md` under `## [Unreleased]`, eliminating the merge-conflict hotspot that bit 50% of parallel subagents in 2026-04-17 pass-1. Release-cut consumes and groups fragments into `## [X.Y.Z]` at bump time. `draft-changelog-entry` and `bump` skills updated accordingly (`parallel-pr-changelog-append-friction`). |
| Backlog sync | Close row: `parallel-pr-changelog-append-friction`. |

---

### 15. `compilation-prewarm-on-load` ‚Äî Opt-in `workspace_warm` MCP tool

**Status:** pending ¬∑ **Order:** 15 ¬∑ **Correctness class:** P4 ¬∑ **Schedule hint:** ‚Äî ¬∑ **Estimated context:** 55000 tokens ¬∑ **CHANGELOG category:** Added

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `compilation-prewarm-on-load` |
| Diagnosis | Multiple tools suffer 100√ó cold-start penalties (first `symbol_search` 4669ms vs 40ms warm). User preference (2026-04-14): cold-start is acceptable; **do NOT** add aggressive auto-prewarm that blocks tool registration. Deps row (`concurrent-mcp-instances-no-tools`) is now SHIPPED (PR #281, in `[Unreleased]` of `CHANGELOG.md`) so the row is unblocked. Scope is opt-in: a new `workspace_warm(workspaceId, projects?)` tool that callers invoke explicitly; no auto-prewarm. |
| Approach | Rule 3 new-MCP-tool structural-unit exemption applies (4 units, typically 5‚Äì7 files). (a) **Core contract:** `src/RoslynMcp.Core/Services/IWorkspaceWarmService.cs` + `src/RoslynMcp.Core/Models/WorkspaceWarmResult.cs` carrying `{ workspaceId, projectsWarmed: [..], elapsedMs, coldCompilationCount }`. (b) **Roslyn implementation:** `src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/WorkspaceWarmService.cs` ‚Äî for each project in the workspace (or the provided filter), call `project.GetCompilationAsync()` to force compile, then `compilation.GetSemanticModel(doc)` on one doc per project to stabilize the semantic model cache. Return the result shape. (c) **Host.Stdio tool surface:** `src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Tools/WorkspaceWarmTools.cs` with `[McpServerTool]` `workspace_warm` wrapping the service. (d) **Registration:** `ServerSurfaceCatalog.cs` entry (stable: false, isRead: false, category `workspace`) + `ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs` DI line. One test file `tests/RoslynMcp.Tests/WorkspaceWarmServiceTests.cs` covering warmed-projects-count + elapsed-ms > 0 on first call, < first on second. |
| Scope | prod: 5‚Äì7 structural-unit files (Rule 3 new-MCP-tool exemption): `IWorkspaceWarmService.cs`, `WorkspaceWarmResult.cs`, `WorkspaceWarmService.cs`, `WorkspaceWarmTools.cs`, `ServerSurfaceCatalog.cs` (entry append), `ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs` (DI line). Rule 3 structural-unit exemption is cited explicitly in the initiative's Scope field (here). tests: +1 (`tests/RoslynMcp.Tests/WorkspaceWarmServiceTests.cs`). |
| Tool policy | `edit-only` |
| Estimated context cost | 55000 tokens |
| Risks | `GetCompilationAsync()` can hold the write-preview gate in shared workspaces ‚Äî use the read lock in `WorkspaceExecutionGate` and return a clear "warm skipped ‚Äî concurrent write in progress" note rather than blocking. Second risk: warming all projects for a large solution can take 30+ seconds ‚Äî the tool is opt-in but the caller may not expect that; return `elapsedMs` so clients can budget. Third risk: `ServerSurfaceCatalog.cs` append is a known hotspot for parallel merge conflicts (see deferred `server-surface-catalog-append-conflict-hotspot`) ‚Äî this initiative is scheduled as the SOLE catalog-touching initiative in this sweep's wave (per `backlog-sweep-execute.md` Step 2a disjoint-production-files rule). |
| Validation | New test asserts: first call returns `projectsWarmed.Count > 0` + `elapsedMs > 100`; second call on same workspace returns `elapsedMs < first / 10`. Manual repro: `workspace_load test.sln` ‚Üí `workspace_warm` ‚Üí `symbol_search` takes <100ms (vs the 4669ms cold baseline). Existing 600+ tests pass. |
| Performance review | Hot-path adjacent: this tool IS the performance fix. Benchmark before/after on the audit-session solution: first `symbol_search` post-`workspace_warm` should drop from ~4s to <100ms. Include benchmark numbers in the PR description. |
| CHANGELOG category | Added |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Added:** `workspace_warm(workspaceId, projects?)` MCP tool ‚Äî opt-in prewarm that forces Roslyn `GetCompilationAsync` + semantic-model resolution across the workspace (or a filtered project set), cutting the first-`symbol_search` latency from ~4600ms to <100ms for callers that invoke it after `workspace_load`. Never runs automatically (per the 2026-04-14 user preference against blocking tool registration). Three-layer shape: `IWorkspaceWarmService` / `WorkspaceWarmResult` (Core), `WorkspaceWarmService` (Roslyn), `WorkspaceWarmTools` (Host.Stdio), catalog + DI (`compilation-prewarm-on-load`). |
| Backlog sync | Close row: `compilation-prewarm-on-load`. Update related: `workspace-process-pool-or-daemon` (now fully unblocked for its own sweep). |
